mission.title: The Doubling Mixboard
mission.story: You are the lead sound engineer at Amplitude Studios. Every time you flip a switch on the new mixboard, the volume doubles — that is the power of exponents. The headline act goes on in one hour, and you must master powers before the speakers can be safely dialed in.
mission.objective: Evaluate powers as repeated multiplication so you can predict the volume at every switch flip.

scenes.name: Sound Check
scenes.text: Flip the switch and watch the volume double from 1 unit to 2³ = 8 units.

scenes.name: Console Terms
scenes.text: Learn the studio's vocabulary: base, exponent, power, evaluate, coefficient, like terms.

scenes.name: Gain Stage
scenes.text: Evaluate each power as repeated multiplication to set the right gain.

scenes.name: Mixing Session
scenes.text: Work the power problems. The console adapts the difficulty to keep you in the pocket.

scenes.name: Track Bounce
scenes.text: Use powers to predict how file size grows as you double the track layers.

scenes.name: Final Mix
scenes.text: Lock the master fader and show the studio you command exponents.

simulation.instructions: Model the gain expression 2x + 3 with tiles, then evaluate it when the volume knob is set to x. Drag two x-tiles and three unit-tiles into the workspace.

finale.title: Master Mix Locked!
finale.text: The volume hit the perfect level and the show was a sellout. Amplitude Studios just made you head engineer — because you can evaluate any power in your sleep.

vocabulary.definition: A number that tells how many times to use the base as a factor
vocabulary.visual: In 2³, the small 3 means multiply 2 by itself 3 times: 2 × 2 × 2 = 8

vocabulary.definition: The number that is used as a repeated factor
vocabulary.visual: In 5², the base is 5 — it is the number being multiplied: 5 × 5 = 25

vocabulary.definition: An expression written with a base and an exponent
vocabulary.visual: 10³ = 10 × 10 × 10 = 1,000 — read as '10 to the third power' or '10 cubed'

vocabulary.definition: To find the value of a numerical or algebraic expression
vocabulary.visual: Evaluate 3⁴: write 3 × 3 × 3 × 3, then multiply step by step: 9 × 9 = 81

vocabulary.definition: The number multiplied by a variable
vocabulary.visual: In 3x, the 3 is the coefficient — if x = 4, then 3x = 3 × 4 = 12

vocabulary.definition: Terms with the same variable raised to the same power
vocabulary.visual: 4x and 2x are like terms (both have x); 4x and 4x² are NOT like terms (different powers)

launch.narrative: You're a sound engineer at a music studio. Each time you flip a switch, the volume doubles. The starting volume is 1 unit. After flipping the switch 3 times, the volume is 2³ = 2 × 2 × 2 = 8 units — that's 8 times louder! How loud does it get after more flips?
launch.contextImage: Music studio mixing board with volume meters doubling at each switch
noticePrompts: What happens to the volume each time you flip a switch?
How is repeated multiplication different from repeated addition?
wonderPrompts: What if the volume tripled each time instead of doubled?
How many flips would it take to reach over 1,000 volume units?

explore.instructions: Evaluate each exponential expression. Write the repeated multiplication and find the value.

discourse.prompt: How does the value change when you increase the exponent by 1? Why?
discourse.sentenceFrame: When the exponent increases by 1, the value is multiplied by ___ because the base is used as a factor ___ more time.

approaching.stem: Which expression shows 5³ as repeated multiplication?
approaching.explanation: 5³ means use 5 as a factor 3 times: 5 × 5 × 5. The exponent tells how many times to multiply, not what to multiply by.

approaching.instructions: Write each power as repeated multiplication, then find the value.

onLevel.instructions: Evaluate each exponential expression. Show the repeated multiplication and the final value.
Sort each power by whether its value is greater than 50 or not.

onLevel.label: Match each power to its value.

onLevel.instructions: Which side is greater? Evaluate each expression. Left: 3⁴. Right: 4³.
onLevel.label: Compare: 3⁴ vs. 4³

extending.title: Find the Exponent Error
extending.correctWork: 2³ means 2 × 2 × 2, NOT 2 × 3. The exponent tells us to multiply the base by itself 3 times: 2 × 2 × 2 = 8.
hints: Does the exponent mean to multiply the base BY the exponent, or to multiply the base BY ITSELF that many times?
Write out what 2³ means as repeated multiplication: 2 × ___ × ___.

extending.prompt: A bacteria colony doubles every hour. Starting with 1 bacterium, write a power expression for the number after 6 hours. Explain why exponential growth is so much faster than adding the same number each hour.
extending.sentenceFrame: After 6 hours there are ___ bacteria because 2⁶ = ___. If it grew by adding 2 each hour instead, there would only be ___ bacteria. Exponential growth is faster because ___.

extending.instructions: Compare the values when the base and exponent are swapped. What do you notice?

connect.scenario: A music studio is recording tracks. Each track layer doubles the file size. A single track is 4 MB. The file size after n layers is given by 4 × 2ⁿ.
connect.promptQuestion: How large is the file after 5 layers? Why does the file size grow so quickly?
connect.prompt: After 5 layers the file is ___ MB because 4 × 2⁵ = 4 × ___ = ___. The file grows quickly because each layer ___ the size.
